channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US on alert for election interference from Russia, China, Iran
item.description: Microsoft said in a report Wednesday that foreign adversaries have shown continued determination to influence the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Navalny widow to Americans: Don't take democracy for granted
item.description: Yulia Navalnaya, the widow of the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny, encouraged Americans not to take democracy for granted, in an interview on ABC’s “The View” that aired Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Mozambique's ruling party candidate declared winner of presidential election as rigging claims swirl
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's ruling party candidate Daniel Chapo was declared the winner of the country's presidential election Thursday as the opposition claimed the vote was rigged and one party said it would challenge the result in court.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentine police raid the Buenos Aires hotel where One Direction’s Liam Payne died
item.description: The police have raided the Buenos Aires hotel where ex-One Direction singer Liam Payne stayed before dying last week after falling from a third-floor balcony.
item.dc:creator: Almudena Calatrava, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian lawmakers ratify pact with North Korea as US confirms that Pyongyang sent troops to Russia
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian lawmakers on Thursday ratified a pact with North Korea envisioning mutual military assistance, a move that comes as the U.S. confirmed the deployment of 3,000 North Korean troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Trash carried by a North Korean balloon fell on the presidential compound in central Seoul on Thursday for the second time, raising concerns about the vulnerability of key South Korean sites.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Paris conference for Lebanon raises $1 billion in pledges for humanitarian and military support
item.description: PARIS (AP) — An international conference for Lebanon in Paris on Thursday raised $1 billion in pledges for humanitarian aid and military support to help the country where war between Hezbollah militants and Israel has displaced a million people, killed over 2,500, and deepened an economic crisis, Fr
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A lawmaker from the far-right Freedom Party is elected as speaker of Austria's parliament
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — A lawmaker from the far-right Freedom Party was elected Thursday as the new speaker of Austria's parliament, weeks after the party won a national election for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza shelter kills 17 as Blinken says cease-fire talks will resume
item.description: Palestinian officials say an Israeli strike on a school where displaced people were sheltering in the central Gaza Strip has killed at least 17 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Putin ends BRICS summit that sought to expand Russia's global clout but was shadowed by Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has presided at the closing session of a summit of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, hailing its role as a counterbalance to the West.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Climate-threatened Pacific Islands criticize Australia at Commonwealth meeting
item.description: APIA, Samoa (AP) — Several Pacific island nations singled out Australia to do more to phase out fossil fuel exports during the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting which opened in Samoa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day
item.description: Turkey has struck suspected Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day after a deadly attack on the premises of a key defense company.
item.dc:creator: Suzan Fraser, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man pleads guilty to displaying a Nazi symbol at pro-Palestinian rally
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — A restaurateur pleaded guilty Thursday to displaying a Nazi symbol during a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney in early October. Alan Yazbek was photographed at the Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive displacement from Israel-Hezbollah war transforms Beirut’s famed commercial street
item.description: Inside what was once one of Beirut’s oldest and best-known cinemas, dozens of Lebanese, Palestinians and Syrians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah war now spend their time following the news on their phones, cooking, chatting or walking around to pass the time.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US confirms North Korea has sent 3,000 troops to Russia for training and possible Ukraine combat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. said Wednesday that 3,000 North Korean troops have been deployed to Russia and are training at several locations, calling the move very serious and warning that those forces will be “fair game” if they go into combat in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes pound Lebanese coastal city after residents evacuate
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli jets struck multiple buildings in Lebanon's southern coastal city of Tyre on Wednesday, sending up large clouds of black smoke, while Hezbollah confirmed that a top official widely expected to be the militant group's next leader had been killed in an Israeli strike.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMED ZAATARI and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel issues travel warning to parts of Sri Lanka over terrorism threat
item.description: Israel's national security council has called on Israelis to leave parts of southern Sri Lanka immediately over the threat of a potential terrorist attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Liberal lawmakers ask Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau not to run for a fourth term
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Some lawmakers in Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's own Liberal Party asked the leader not to run for a fourth term Wednesday, handing him one of the biggest tests of his political career.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican troops kill 19 suspected cartel members, suffer no casualties: officials
item.description: Mexican army troops have reportedly killed 19 suspected cartel affiliates during a Monday shootout near Culiacan, the state capital of Sinaloa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Situation in Haiti worsens amid ‘acute violence’ as UN support mission falters under gang violence
item.description: The gang violence in Haiti has gotten worse despite the deployment of U.N.-sanctioned international police officers, forcing mass displacement, hunger among millions and even famine for thousands of Haitians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explosion at Turkish aerospace facility ruled a terror attack
item.description: Turkish authorities say an explosion at a defense aerospace facility on Wednesday was a terrorist attack that killed an unknown number of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea has sent troops to Russia, Pentagon confirms
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin confirmed that North Korean troops have been transferred to Russia on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon chief: North Korean troops are in Russia
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin said North Korean troops appear to be in Russia. “We are seeing evidence that there are North Korean troops that have, that have gone to … Russia,” Austin told reporters in Rome on Wednesday. “What exactly they’re doing, left to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hezbollah confirms death of senior commander in line to replace Hassan Nasrallah
item.description: The Israeli military eliminated yet another top commander within Hezbollah this month, as Secretary of State Antony Blinken met with Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: US scrambles after Israel war plan leak: What to know
item.description: An apparent U.S. intelligence breach related to Israel's plans to strike Iran has unsettled the intelligence community and sent the Biden administration scrambling to determine if it came from a hack or an internal leak.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken urges Netanyahu to seek truces in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday to discuss ending the conflict in Lebanon and Gaza in addition to securing pathways for humanitarian aid, according to a readout from the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Woman becomes stuck upside down between boulders for 7 hours while trying to retrieve dropped phone
item.description: A woman who became trapped in between two boulders in Australia after falling into a crevice while trying to retrieve her phone has been saved, NSW Ambulance says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy says a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway Germany
item.description: Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his “victory plan” after the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken arrives in Israel as Biden-Harris admin faces scrutiny over compromising Jerusalem’s security
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken arrived for talks with Israel on his 11th trip there since October 2023. The Jewish state is fighting a seven-front war against Iran-backed proxies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peru’s ex-president gets 20+ years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing student in Spain was likely crushed, incinerated after passing out in trash while partying: cops
item.description: A student missing in Majorca, Spain, was likely crushed to death in a garbage truck before she was incinerated at a waste disposal plant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China holds live-fire drills opposite Taiwan week after large-scale exercise
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China is holding live-fire drills off the coast of its southern Fujian province facing Taiwan, just a week after a massive air-and-sea drill it described as punishment for Taiwan's president rejecting Beijing's claims of sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Italian surfer dies in freak accident after swordfish impales her while surfing in Indonesia
item.description: An Italian surfer hunting for waves off the west coast of Sumatra died after she was stabbed in the chest by the bill of a swordfish, in what is being considered a freak accident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9
